reference: When someone lies down after a heavy meal, there is reflux of food from the stomach to the esophagus if he has a lax gastroesophageal sphincter. I would suggest a scan for hiatus hernia, which means the stomach herniating into the chest, which also causes chest pain, and also an endoscopy. I suggest he has light meals and does not lie down soon after eating.
text: When someone lies down after a heavy meal, there's reflux of food from the stomach to the esophagus. If he has a lax gastroesophageal sphincter, I would suggest a scan for hiatus hernia, which means the stomach herniating into the chest, which also causes chest pain, and also an endoscopy. I suggest he has light meals and does not lie down soon after eating.
